question: [File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a large params array to getContractLogsQuery() where params.push(limit) appends to an already large array, and buildSelectQuery() further pushes filter values for each of lower/inner/upper subqueries, causing the params array to grow unboundedly and exhaust Node.js heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the three-subquery UNION path in getContractLogsQuery() where each of the lower, inner, and upper filter arrays causes a separate full execution of contractLogsExtendedQuery including the entity CTE, resulting in three independent full-table-scan queries that together exhaust database I/O and cause a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: static contractLogsExtendedQuery] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated execution of contractLogsExtendedQuery which contains an inline entity CTE (select evm_address, id from entity) that performs a full scan of the entity table on every query execution, causing sustained disk I/O exhaustion and a non-network DoS when called at high frequency?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: static contractStateTimestampQuery] Can an unprivileged external user trigger contractStateTimestampQuery which uses DISTINCT ON (slot) requiring PostgreSQL to sort the entire contract_state_change table by slot and consensus_timestamp, and with no upper bound on the number of rows processed, cause sustained CPU and memory exhaustion in the database constituting a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: static contractStateChangesQuery] Can an unprivileged external user trigger contractStateChangesQuery which performs a LEFT JOIN between contract_state_change and the entity table without any index hint, causing a full nested-loop join on large tables that exhausts PostgreSQL CPU and memory and constitutes a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: static contractStateQuery] Can an unprivileged external user trigger contractStateQuery which performs a LEFT JOIN between contract_state and entity tables inside a CTE, and with no WHERE clause restrictions applied before the join, cause PostgreSQL to perform a full cross-table join that exhausts database memory and constitutes a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractActions()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a filters array where every entry has key === 'index' to getContractActions(), causing the for loop to execute params.push(filter.value) and string concatenation of whereClause thousands of times, blocking the Node.js event loop through synchronous CPU-intensive string building and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractActionsByConsensusTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user call getContractActionsByConsensusTimestamp() with a valid consensusTimestamp and payerAccountId but pass an unbounded filters array, causing getContractActions() to build an enormous query string and params array that exhausts both Node.js and PostgreSQL memory in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResultsByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a single-element timestamps value (non-array) to getContractResultsByTimestamps() where params = [timestamps] and timestampsOpAndValue = '= $1', and then pass an enormous involvedContractIds array causing the WHERE clause to contain a massive IN list that exhausts PostgreSQL query parsing resources?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger getContractLogs() to return a result set where all rows have unique consensus_timestamp values, causing the deduplication loop (rows.forEach) to push every timestamp into the timestamps array, and then RecordFileService.getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestampArray() to issue a secondary query with thousands of parameters, causing a cascading non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger getContractLogs() to return a large rows array, causing rows.map((cr) => new ContractLog(cr, recordFileMap.get(cr.consensus_timestamp))) to instantiate thousands of ContractLog objects simultaneously, each using Object.assign with mapKeys camelCase transformation, exhausting Node.js heap memory and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResultsByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a large timestamps array to getContractResultsByTimestamps() causing rows.map((row) => ({...new ContractResult(row), evmAddress: row.evm_address})) to spread-copy thousands of ContractResult objects, each containing large binary fields like call_result and function_parameters, exhausting Node.js heap memory in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractStateChangesByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger getContractStateChangesByTimestamps() to return a large result set, causing rows.map((row) => ({...new ContractStateChange(row), evmAddress: row.evm_address})) to instantiate and spread-copy thousands of objects simultaneously, exhausting Node.js heap memory and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResultsByIdAndFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a large whereParams array to getContractResultsByIdAndFilters() where params.push(limit) appends to an already large array, and the resulting query with many bind parameters causes PostgreSQL to allocate excessive memory for parameter binding, constituting a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: static ethereumTransactionByPayerAndTimestampArrayQuery] Can an unprivileged external user pass large payers and timestamps arrays to getEthereumTransactionsByPayerAndTimestampArray() where the query uses payer_account_id = any($1) and consensus_timestamp = any($2) with array parameters, causing PostgreSQL to perform a full sequential scan with array membership checks on every row, exhausting database CPU in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEthereumTransactionsByPayerAndTimestampArray()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a timestamps array containing MAX_LONG BigInt values to getEthereumTransactionsByPayerAndTimestampArray() causing the minTimestamp/maxTimestamp computation loop to process BigInt comparisons for thousands of entries, blocking the Node.js event loop and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a conditions array with many entries to getContractLogsQuery() where buildSelectQuery() concatenates all conditions into a WHERE clause for each of the three UNION subqueries, tripling the size of the generated SQL string and causing PostgreSQL to spend excessive CPU parsing the oversized query?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the UNION path in getContractLogsQuery() where the final query is [subQueries.map((q) => '(' + q + ')').join('\\nunion\\n'), orderClauseNoAlias, limitClause].join('\\n'), producing a query string that is three times the size of a single subquery, causing PostgreSQL to allocate excessive memory for query parsing and planning?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: static contractResultsWithEvmAddressQuery] Can an unprivileged external user trigger contractResultsWithEvmAddressQuery which selects all contractResultsFields including large binary columns (call_result, function_parameters, failed_initcode, bloom) without any size restriction, causing the database to return rows with large binary payloads that exhaust Node.js heap memory during result mapping?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResultsByIdAndFiltersQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an empty whereConditions array to getContractResultsByIdAndFiltersQuery() causing the query to omit the WHERE clause entirely and perform a full table scan of contract_result joined with entity, returning a result set bounded only by the limit parameter, and if the limit is large, exhausting database I/O and Node.js memory?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractStateByIdAndFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user call getContractStateByIdAndFilters() with timestamp=true and an empty whereConditions array, causing contractStateTimestampQuery with DISTINCT ON (slot) to scan the entire contract_state_change table and perform an expensive sort operation, exhausting PostgreSQL work_mem and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractStateByIdAndFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user call getContractStateByIdAndFilters() with timestamp=true causing the ORDER BY to include both ContractStateChange.SLOT and ContractStateChange.CONSENSUS_TIMESTAMP DESC, requiring PostgreSQL to sort by two columns on a large dataset, doubling the sort memory requirement and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractActions()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a filters array with duplicate index filter entries to getContractActions() causing the WHERE clause to contain redundant AND conditions that force PostgreSQL to evaluate the same index predicate multiple times, wasting CPU and causing degraded performance constituting a non-network DoS?
